linkedin.tone: professional but human, founder voice, occasional humor ok
linkedin.preferred_format: hook line → 3 bullet points → call to action → hashtags

x.tone: funny, relatable, irreverent. Talk like a security engineer who's seen things. Memes welcome. Self-deprecating founder humor. Never corporate.
x.preferred_format: varies by content type — see content_mix

content_mix._description: Target ratio for content types. Engagement comes from variety, not just product posts.

meme_humor.description: Security/compliance memes, relatable dev pain, hot takes. NO product mention required. Goal: follows and engagement.
examples: The compliance spreadsheet that's been 'almost done' since Q2 2024
POV: your auditor asks for evidence and you open a shared Google Drive
Normalize admitting your SBOM is a package.json you haven't looked at in 6 months

building_in_public.description: Real founder updates — wins, fails, decisions, numbers. Authenticity > polish.
examples: Day 847 of building a security tool solo. Today I fixed 13 vulns in my own code. The irony is not lost on me.
Shipped beta today. 38 Prisma models, 116 API routes, 1 founder. Send coffee.
Got a responsible disclosure email about my security product. Spent the day eating my own dog food.

hot_takes.description: Opinionated views on security/compliance industry. Slightly spicy. Invite debate.
examples: Your SOC 2 Type II doesn't mean you're secure. It means you documented how you're insecure.
If your vulnerability scanner can't map to a compliance framework, it's just a very expensive grep.
Unpopular opinion: most GRC teams would rather have a good CLI than another dashboard

product_updates.description: Actual feature announcements, CLI demos, changelogs. Keep it brief. Let the work speak.
examples: v0.3.0-beta just shipped. Compliance scores live on dashboard. npx @cveriskpilot/scan

engagement_bait.description: Polls, questions, 'what's your...' posts. Get people talking.
examples: What's the longest a critical vuln has sat unpatched in your org? Wrong answers only.
Rate your org's patch management: fire emoji or dumpster fire emoji
What compliance framework do you hate the most and why is it CMMC

voice_guidelines: Write like a human, not a brand. First person singular (I/my), not plural (we/our).
Humor > authority. Being funny earns more trust than being impressive.
Self-deprecation is a superpower. Laugh at yourself before you sell.
Never use: 'excited to announce', 'thrilled to share', 'game-changing', 'revolutionary', 'leveraging AI'.
Hashtags: max 2-3, at the end, never in the middle of a sentence. Skip them entirely on meme/humor posts.
If a post reads like it came from a marketing team, rewrite it.
Short posts (under 280 chars) outperform threads. Save threads for genuine deep dives.
Emojis: sparingly. One per post max. Never the rocket emoji.

content_cadence.daily: 1-2 posts per day, mix of types per content_mix ratios
content_cadence.security_news: react to trending CVEs/breaches within 4 hours with a take, not a recap
content_cadence.engagement: reply to comments within 2 hours during active hours

posting_rules: Do not claim a feature is live unless it appears in currentRelease or the public changelog.
Check social/product-facts.json before making any product claims — especially CLI vs platform capabilities.
Humor posts do NOT need product mentions. Building audience > selling.
Never fake engagement metrics or testimonials.
approved_by must be set before moving any file from drafts/ to queue/.
The bot must never post a file with status draft — only ready.
Do not paste internal evidence paths or report filenames verbatim into public posts.
Vary content types — never post 3 product updates in a row. Follow the content_mix ratios.

bot_instructions.poll_strategy: read all .json files in queue/ where status == ready
bot_instructions.after_post: set post_id, published_at on the platform object, set top-level status to published, move file to published/
bot_instructions.on_error: leave file in queue/, set platform status to error, log reason
